description_long: This is a large, rustic log cabin with a complex, multi-gabled roof structure covered in brown shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from thick, rounded logs, giving it a traditional log cabin appearance, while stonework is visible at the base and around the chimney. The front facade features a prominent covered porch with timber railings and exposed beams, flanked by two wings that extend outward. Multiple windows of varying sizes, framed in green, are distributed asymmetrically across the building, providing ample natural light. The roof includes several dormers and skylights, adding architectural interest and functionality. The overall design suggests a spacious, multi-room residence suitable for a forested or mountainous urban edge.
description: A large, rustic log cabin with a multi-gabled shingle roof, stone base, and prominent covered porch, featuring numerous green-framed windows and timber details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch with timber railings, and two projecting wings with visible windows and roof details.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the cabin, a large gabled section, and multiple green-framed windows along the side wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a central chimney, several windows, and a smaller covered entryway, with the log construction clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing another gabled wing, stonework at the base, and additional windows, with a smaller porch-like structure.
attribute_roof_type: Complex multi-gabled roof with intersecting gables, dormers, and wide overhangs, covered in brown shingles.
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple small to medium-sized rectangular windows, asymmetrically distributed across all visible facades, with some grouped in pairs and others spaced individually; green window frames provide a distinctive accent.
attribute_door_placement: One main entrance door centered on the front facade beneath the porch, and a secondary door at the rear under a smaller covered entryway.
